title: Head of Revenue Operations
description: Finout, a cloud cost management platform trusted by The New York Times, Wiz, Elastic, and Lyft, seeks a Head of Revenue Operations to build and scale its GTM operational backbone. Reporting to the CRO, this hands-on leadership role requires 7+ years in RevOps, Sales Ops, or Strategy within high-growth B2B SaaS, experience scaling beyond $30M+ ARR, deep Salesforce administration expertise, and strong cross-functional leadership across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR. Involves collaboration with the Tel Aviv HQ.
score_reason: Exceptional match: Omri's 15+ years in B2B SaaS RevOps, Salesforce expertise, and GTM scaling experience directly align with Finout's need for a Head of RevOps to build operational infrastructure. Tel Aviv/New York hybrid location perfectly fits his geographic preferences.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years scaling RevOps in high-growth B2B SaaS environments, and Finout's mission to bring operational rigor to cloud cost management resonates deeply. Your GTM challenge of orchestrating Sales, Marketing, and Finance around a rapidly scaling platform is exactly the kind of operational scaling I've built careers solving—and I'm particularly drawn to doing this from the Tel Aviv hub.

title: Sr. RevOps Program Manager
description: Engine, a fast-growing travel and fintech platform backed by Blackstone and Permira serving 20,000+ companies, seeks a Senior RevOps Program Manager to own the operational rhythm of its go-to-market engine. The role owns the RevOps program portfolio end-to-end—GTM launches, system rollouts, process changes, and enablement initiatives—and builds cross-functional operating cadences. Requires 5+ years in program management, RevOps, sales operations, or GTM operations, partnering with Sales Leadership, Finance, and Product to translate priorities into structured workstreams.
score_reason: Exceptional alignment with Omri's 15+ years B2B SaaS RevOps expertise, fintech industry experience, and program management capabilities. The Sr. RevOps PM role perfectly matches his GTM operations, process optimization, and cross-functional orchestration background.
ai_opener: I've been following Engine's impressive growth as a Blackstone/Permira-backed fintech platform—your focus on operational excellence in GTM resonates deeply with my work scaling revenue operations across hypergrowth SaaS companies. With 15+ years building RevOps programs from scratch, including GTM launches and cross-functional operating cadences in fintech environments, I'm genuinely excited about owning the operational rhythm that powers your 20,000+ company network.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: Gong, a leader in revenue intelligence, seeks a Revenue Operations Manager focused on Territory Strategy & Operations to accelerate revenue growth and seller productivity through data-driven investment strategy and scalable territory design. Responsibilities include redesigning territory models for global scalability, partnering with Sales leadership, Strategy, and Finance to optimize territory composition, and providing dashboards and regional insights. Requires strong cross-functional collaboration and comfort navigating ambiguity in a fast-paced environment. Salary range $149,000-$175,000.
score_reason: Exceptional match for a senior RevOps executive—territory strategy, sales operations optimization, and cross-functional GTM leadership are core competencies. Gong.io's revenue intelligence focus and global scaling challenges align perfectly with Omri's pipeline forecasting and B2B SaaS operations background.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years architecting revenue operations and GTM infrastructure for scaling B2B SaaS companies, with particular depth in territory modeling, sales forecasting, and data-driven pipeline optimization. Your Territory Strategy & Operations role caught my attention because redesigning territory models for global scalability is exactly the kind of cross-functional, high-leverage problem I've solved repeatedly—and I'm impressed by how Gong is using revenue intelligence to drive seller productivity.

title: Senior RevOps Processes Specialist
description: Remote, a global HR platform enabling compliant international employment, seeks a Senior RevOps Process Specialist to lead sales process improvements, streamline operations, and implement scalable solutions. The role collaborates cross-functionally to improve integration between Salesforce and systems like HubSpot, Leandata, and Vitally, monitors process adoption and data governance, and maintains data integrity within customer records. Fully remote, globally distributed, with base salary range of $40,900-$92,050 USD depending on location.
score_reason: Strong alignment with Omri's RevOps expertise, Salesforce/HubSpot proficiency, and process optimization background. Remote global role matches location preferences perfectly, though the salary range appears below market for a 15+ year senior executive.
ai_opener: Hi, I came across Remote's mission to simplify global employment compliance and was impressed by your focus on Salesforce-HubSpot integration and data governance. With 15+ years scaling revenue operations in B2B SaaS, I've led similar cross-functional process improvements and implemented the exact tech stack you're standardizing—I'd welcome exploring how I could help Remote operationalize its growth engine.

title: RevOps Manager
description: Platomics, a healthcare technology company, seeks a Revenue Operations Manager to be the backbone of its revenue engine. The role involves designing, optimizing, and documenting workflows across sales, marketing, and customer success; building dashboards and analyzing funnel metrics; administering the CRM (HubSpot) and RevOps tools; and supporting forecasting, territory planning, and quota setting. Certifications in Salesforce or HubSpot preferred. Remote flexibility anywhere in Europe with competitive salary of €25,000-€31,000 annually.
score_reason: Strong technical alignment (HubSpot, RevOps, B2B SaaS fundamentals) but significant overqualification concerns—Omri's 15+ years as senior executive targets Head of Revenue/GTM roles, not individual contributor manager positions. Compensation (€25-31k) is substantially below market for his seniority level.
ai_opener: Hi team—I'm impressed by Platomics' focus on healthcare tech RevOps infrastructure. My 15+ years building revenue engines across B2B SaaS (including HubSpot-native GTM stacks) suggests I could architect your workflows and dashboarding at a more strategic level. Open to discussing how I might accelerate your revenue operations maturity.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Pylon seeks a GTM Engineer to test, implement, and optimize an AI outbound solution, build and manage automated outbound sequences with high deliverability, and own email marketing infrastructure including domain health and warming strategies. The role creates data pipelines between marketing tools, CRM, and sales tooling, collaborates with RevOps on lead scoring and routing, and ships growth experiments like website widgets and ROI calculators. Requires 3+ years in marketing operations or GTM/growth engineering at B2B SaaS companies, with strong API and webhook skills.
score_reason: Strong alignment with Omri's RevOps/GTM background, AI-native GTM expertise, and data pipeline/automation skills. The role perfectly bridges his RevOps strengths with growth engineering, though it skews slightly more technical/execution-focused than typical Head of RevOps positions.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years building GTM engines at B2B SaaS companies, and Pylon's approach to AI-driven outbound infrastructure caught my attention. I've led similar initiatives around email deliverability, lead scoring, and RevOps/Growth collaboration, and I'm particularly interested in how you're thinking about data pipeline integrity at scale with your AI outbound solution.

title: GTM Engineer
description: FacilityOS, a B2B SaaS company providing a facility management platform for mid-to-large enterprises, seeks a GTM Engineer to build, configure, and continuously improve a collection of intelligent AI agents that help Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success work smarter and faster. Working at the intersection of data, automation, and human workflows, you'll design how their 'AI brain' learns and operates in the real world, focusing on both how the AI systems connect and run and how they improve over time.
score_reason: Strong match on role (GTM Engineer), company stage (B2B SaaS), and AI-native GTM expertise. Perfect alignment with Omri's RevOps background building intelligent systems for Sales/Marketing/CS workflows and pipeline optimization.
ai_opener: FacilityOS is solving a massive pain point—facility teams drowning in disconnected data and manual workflows. Your AI agents approach reminds me of the RevOps automation stacks that compress sales cycles by 30-40%. I've spent 15 years building exactly these kinds of intelligent workflows across Salesforce/HubSpot ecosystems, and I'd be interested in exploring how to architect your AI brain to actually move deals and reduce CAC.

title: GTM Engineer (Remote)
description: Nooks, an AI Sales Assistant Platform backed by $70M from top VCs including Kleiner Perkins and loved by sales teams at HubSpot and Rippling, seeks its first GTM Engineer to serve as the technical co-pilot for its Sales Development organization. The role owns setup, optimization, and enablement within the Nooks platform. ai_opener: Hi Omri, I came across Nooks' impressive growth story (backed by Kleiner Perkins, loved by HubSpot and Rippling sales teams) and thought of you immediately. Your 15+ years in B2B SaaS RevOps and deep Salesforce/HubSpot expertise would be perfect for scaling their GTM function as their first GTM Engineer—especially as they execute their next 3x growth phase.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Conveo is an AI research platform enabling fast, affordable, high-quality consumer/B2B research, with global brands like Unilever, Google, and Orange using its AI video interviewer. Scaling 20x revenue in 2026, Conveo seeks a GTM Engineer who can code, ship, and iterate on internal tools and automations to make the GTM team highly effective—replacing manual workflows with purpose-built apps, pipelines, and AI-powered workflows like learning lead scoring models, attribution pipelines, onboarding flows, and AI agents.
score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer title, AI-native platform, and RevOps automation focus. Slight concern: Conveo is B2B research (not traditional SaaS product company), requires coding/engineering skills (Omri's primary strength is RevOps strategy), and no mention of product-market fit stage or current ARR, making scaling trajectory uncertain.
ai_opener: Omri, Conveo's 20x revenue scaling goal for 2026 requires bulletproof GTM execution—and I noticed you're building internal tools and AI agents to automate lead scoring, attribution, and onboarding. Your 15+ years automating revenue workflows in B2B SaaS could be exactly what their GTM team needs to replace manual processes with purpose-built systems. Worth a conversation about how to scale their GTM operations engine?

title: Head of Growth
description: A high-velocity platform connecting creators and audiences seeks a Head of Growth to lead product-led growth, owning systems, experiments, and analytics that drive activation, retention, monetization, and viral growth. The role builds and mentors a world-class Growth team, establishes KPIs and experimentation infrastructure, and collaborates across product, engineering, data, and marketing. Requires proven experience building end-to-end growth systems, deep understanding of activation/retention/monetization mechanics, and strong analytical skills with Amplitude or Mixpanel.
score_reason: Strong GTM/systems-building background and analytics expertise align well with the growth infrastructure and experimentation focus. However, this is more product-led growth and creator platform-specific than Omri's core B2B SaaS/RevOps positioning, and lacks the fintech/enterprise sales motion he typically excels in.
ai_opener: I noticed Jobgether is scaling a creator-audience connection platform and building out growth infrastructure from first principles. With 15+ years building end-to-end GTM systems in B2B SaaS—from pipeline forecasting to analytics-driven activation loops—I'm intrigued by your focus on experimentation infrastructure and retention mechanics. I'd be curious to discuss how your monetization model is structured and whether you're seeing product-led and sales-led channels both contribute to growth.

title: Head of Growth Marketing
description: A rapidly growing FinTech company seeks a Head of Growth Marketing to shape and execute an innovative growth strategy fueling customer acquisition and revenue generation. The role leads the Demand Gen team across performance marketing and digital channels, owns paid acquisition strategy across search, display, and social media, and collaborates closely with product and sales teams. Requires 6-10+ years in growth, digital, or performance marketing within FinTech or B2B SaaS, 2+ years managing teams, and strong SEO and paid acquisition expertise.
score_reason: Strong FinTech/B2B SaaS background and GTM expertise align well, but this is primarily a performance marketing and demand gen leadership role rather than RevOps/Growth Operations. Omri's RevOps strength is better suited to pipeline/forecasting-focused positions than paid acquisition strategy ownership.
